title: [InstCombine] Enable factorizeMinMaxTree for floating-point values
author: thofry
description:
This patch extends `factorizeMinMaxTree` to floating point types by introducing handling for fast math flags.

The test coverage was copied from the integer case with additional tests for the handling of fast math flags

## Proofs
Proof of base case (times out but runs in <5 mins locally): https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/vt-Kxf
FMF proofs timed out after 12 hours

Manual proof for the `nnan` fast math flag special handling:
Assume WLOG the optimisation is `m1(m2(a, b), m3(c, b)) -> m4(m2(a, b), c)`

-  `minimum/maximum`

`m1` is marked `nnan`
=> results of `m2` and `m3 ` are not `NaN` 
=> `a`, `b`, and `c` are not `NaN` as minimum and maximum return `NaN` when either argument is  `NaN`
=> neither of the arguments to `m4` nor the result can be `NaN`
=> `m4` can be marked `nnan`

Therefore `m4` can safely copy `nnan` from `m1`

- `minnum/minimumnum/maxnum/maximumnum`

`m3` is marked `nnan`
=> `b` and `c` are not `NaN`
=> the result of `m2` is not `NaN` as it has at least one non-`NaN` argument
=> neither of the arguments to `m4` nor the result can be `NaN`
=> `m4` can be marked `nnan`

Therefore `m4` can safely copy `nnan` from `m3`

tests.test_body:
; Function Attrs: nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare float @llvm.maximum.f32(float, float) #0

declare void @use(float)

define float @maximum_reuse_lhs(float %x, float %y, float %z) {
  %m1 = call float @llvm.maximum.f32(float %x, float %y)
  call void @use(float %m1)
  %m2 = call float @llvm.maximum.f32(float %z, float %x)
  %m3 = call float @llvm.maximum.f32(float %m1, float %m2)
  ret float %m3
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

; Function Attrs: nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare float @llvm.maximum.f32(float, float) #0

declare void @use(float)

define float @maximum_reuse_lhs_fmf_intersection(float %x, float %y, float %z) {
  %m1 = call float @llvm.maximum.f32(float %x, float %y)
  call void @use(float %m1)
  %m2 = call ninf afn float @llvm.maximum.f32(float %z, float %x)
  %m3 = call ninf arcp float @llvm.maximum.f32(float %m1, float %m2)
  ret float %m3
}

declare float @llvm.maximum.f32(float, float) #0

declare void @use(float)

define float @neg_neg_extra_use_x(float %x, float %y) {
  %negx = fsub float -0.000000e+00, %x
  %negy = fsub float -0.000000e+00, %y
  %r = call float @llvm.maximum.f32(float %negx, float %negy)
  call void @use(float %negx)
  ret float %r
}

comments.author: github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--LLVM NEW CONTRIBUTOR COMMENT-->

Thank you for submitting a Pull Request (PR) to the LLVM Project!

This PR will be automatically labeled and the relevant teams will be notified.

If you wish to, you can add reviewers by using the "Reviewers" section on this page.

If this is not working for you, it is probably because you do not have write permissions for the repository. In which case you can instead tag reviewers by name in a comment by using `@` followed by their GitHub username.

If you have received no comments on your PR for a week, you can request a review by "ping"ing the PR by adding a comment “Ping”. The common courtesy "ping" rate is once a week. Please remember that you are asking for valuable time from other developers.

If you have further questions, they may be answered by the [LLVM GitHub User Guide](https://llvm.org/docs/GitHub.html).

You can also ask questions in a comment on this PR, on the [LLVM Discord](https://discord.com/invite/xS7Z362) or on the [forums](https://discourse.llvm.org/).
